questions.question: Which CSS property creates a flex container?
questions.explanation: display: flex (or inline-flex) creates a flex container.

questions.question: What is the default value of display for a flex container if not specified?
questions.explanation: display: flex makes the container block-level, but the initial value of display is still block. The flex value must be explicitly set.

questions.question: What happens to float on flex items inside a flex container?
questions.explanation: Floats are ignored on flex items; flex formatting context overrides them.

questions.question: What is the difference between display: flex and display: inline-flex?
options: flex items are vertical in inline-flex
inline-flex makes container an inline-level element
questions.explanation: inline-flex makes the container behave like an inline element while keeping flex behavior.

questions.question: Which property adds spacing between flex items?
questions.explanation: The gap property adds spacing specifically between flex items.

questions.question: What is the initial value of gap on a flex container?
questions.explanation: The initial value of gap is normal, which computes to 0 in flex layouts.

questions.question: Do margins collapse in a flex container?
questions.explanation: Margins on flex items do NOT collapse.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a flex container property?
questions.explanation: flex-grow is a flex item property, not a container property.

questions.question: What effect does vertical-align have on flex items?
questions.explanation: vertical-align has no effect on flex items.

questions.question: What happens if you apply display: flex to an element with float?
options: Float is ignored on the container
questions.explanation: The flex formatting context overrides float on the container itself in some cases.

questions.question: Which gap value creates 16px spacing between flex items?
questions.explanation: gap: 1rem equals 16px (based on root font-size).

questions.question: Can images be flex items?
options: Yes, any direct child can be a flex item
Only if wrapped in a div
questions.explanation: Any direct child of a flex container becomes a flex item, including images, buttons, etc.

questions.question: How does display: inline-flex differ from display: flex?
questions.explanation: inline-flex makes the container shrink-to-fit like an inline element.

questions.question: What is the default initial value of align-items?
questions.explanation: align-items has an initial value of stretch.

questions.question: Can you have a flex container nested inside another flex container?
questions.explanation: Flex containers can be nested (a flex item can also be a flex container).

questions.question: What does gap: 2rem do inside a flex container?
questions.explanation: gap adds spacing BETWEEN flex items, not around them.

questions.question: Which of these creates a flex container that stays on the same line as surrounding text?
questions.explanation: display: inline-flex creates an inline-level flex container.

questions.question: What happens to ::first-line pseudo-element on a flex container?
options: Does not apply to flex containers
questions.explanation: ::first-line pseudo-element does not apply to elements with display: flex.

questions.question: What is the default value of flex-wrap on a flex container?
questions.explanation: The initial value of flex-wrap is nowrap.

questions.question: How do you create a flex container that takes full width of its parent?
questions.explanation: display: flex creates a block-level container that takes full width by default.
